ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Sour Plum Drink (Suanmeitang). Mapped to closest parent class 'fruit beverage (liquid)' as the drink is derived from simmered smoked plums and hawthorn fruit.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fruit and vegetable juices and nectars
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Suanmeitang. A00LC (Fruit and vegetable juices and nectars) is the closest defensible category; this is a traditional simmered fruit-based beverage, not a pressed juice, so the match is approximate.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for Sour Plum Drink.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Sour Plum Drink is approximately 250 ml (one glass), providing around 113 kcal, 28.75 g carbohydrate, and 26.25 g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Plum juice / fruit-based beverage, closest analogous entry; values cross-referenced with published composition data for Suanmeitang ingredients
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for traditional Suanmeitang. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for plum juice (FDC ID 167771), hawthorn berry, and rock sugar, weighted by typical recipe proportions. Input JSON values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.9) were used as a starting point and are consistent with USDA component data. Fat is confirmed negligible (naturally zero) for this water-based beverage with no added fat. Vitamin B12 and Vitamin D are confirmed zero as all ingredients are plant-derived.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of fruit-based beverages with added sugar — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for plum juice and sugar-sweetened fruit drinks
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Suanmeitang exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated by analogy with plum juice (GI ~53, University of Sydney GI Database) and sugar-sweetened fruit beverages (GI ~55–60). The organic acids (malic, tartaric) and polyphenols from smoked plums and hawthorn are known to moderate postprandial glucose response, supporting a low-to-medium GI estimate. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (55 × 28.75) / 100 = 15.8 g, classified as medium GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (5 mg/100 g) with moderate potassium (65 mg/100 g); hawthorn (Shanzha) has traditional cardiovascular-supportive use
hypertension.guidance: Sour Plum Drink is low in sodium and may be consumed in moderation by individuals with hypertension, though the added rock sugar contributes calories and should be accounted for in overall dietary management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Contains 10.5 g sugar per 100 g (predominantly from added rock sugar); GI estimated at 55 (low-medium boundary); a 250 ml serving delivers approximately 26 g sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Sour Plum Drink due to its significant added sugar content; if consumed, portion size should be restricted to no more than 100–125 ml and blood glucose response monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; contains polyphenols and organic acids from smoked plums and hawthorn with antioxidant properties; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sour Plum Drink is fat-free and low in sodium, and the polyphenol-rich ingredients (smoked plum, hawthorn) may offer mild antioxidant benefit; however, the added sugar content should be minimised for individuals managing cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Antioxidants from smoked plums and hawthorn may be beneficial, but high sugar intake can be pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the antioxidant content of Sour Plum Drink but should be mindful that regular consumption of added sugar can promote systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sour Plum Drink (酸梅湯 / 酸梅汤, Suānméitāng) is a classic Chinese herbal beverage with over a thousand years of history, historically associated with the imperial courts of Beijing. It is prepared by simmering smoked plums (Wumei, Prunus mume), dried hawthorn berries (Shanzha), licorice root, dried osmanthus flowers, and rock sugar in water, then straining and chilling the resulting liquid. The drink has a deep burgundy colour and a complex flavour profile that is simultaneously smoky, sweet, sour, and slightly floral. It is traditionally served cold and is prized for its ability to quench thirst, stimulate appetite, and aid digestion, particularly after heavy or fatty meals. Nutritionally, Sour Plum Drink is a low-calorie, low-fat, low-sodium beverage providing approximately 45 kcal per 100 ml, with 11.5 g carbohydrate (predominantly 10.5 g sugar from added rock sugar) and negligible protein and fat. It contains modest amounts of potassium (65 mg/100 g), calcium (8 mg/100 g), and antioxidant polyphenols from its fruit and herb ingredients. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low-medium boundary), with a medium glycemic load per standard 250 ml serving (~15.8 g GL). Clinically, it is suitable for individuals with hypertension and cardiovascular disease in moderate amounts due to its low sodium and fat content, but should be limited or avoided by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its significant added sugar content. It is vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It remains a summer staple throughout China and is widely available in Chinese restaurants and households across Southeast Asia.

Popular as a summer drink and thirst quencher

Common at Chinese banquets and family meals

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Suanmeitang exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for plum juice and constituent ingredients, consistent with the input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using plum juice and sugar-sweetened fruit beverage data. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of a direct approved-source match for this traditional preparation.
notes: fat_g is naturally zero: Sour Plum Drink is a water-based herbal beverage prepared by simmering plant ingredients with no added fat; fat content is negligible and confirmed consistent with USDA FoodData Central plum juice data. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all 0.0 for the same reason. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as all ingredients are plant-derived and no animal products are used. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 as no dietary vitamin D sources are present in the ingredients. water_g estimated at 87.9 g/100 g based on the beverage nature of the product (energy balance cross-check: 0.2 g protein × 4 + 11.5 g carbohydrate × 4 + 0.0 g fat × 9 = 0.8 + 46.0 + 0.0 = 46.8 kcal, consistent with reported 45 kcal within rounding tolerance). Duplicate portion entry in input JSON (two identical '1 serving (250ml)' entries) was deduplicated to a single 250 g serving. Input food_group 'Side dishes' was reclassified to 'Beverages' as Sour Plum Drink is a liquid beverage, not a side dish. GI glycemic load calculation: GI 55 × carbohydrate per serving 28.75 g / 100 = 15.8 g GL per 250 ml serving.
